cases.title: Evans v. Arizona Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE John Evans appealed Arizona Presbytery's vote to divest him of his office without censure under BCO 34-10, after he had already affiliated with the Evangelical Presbyterian Church in England and Wales and notified the Presbytery of his withdrawal. The SJC unanimously sustained the appeal and reversed the Presbytery's action, holding that Evans had withdrawn from Presbytery's jurisdiction under BCO 38-3a before the divestiture proceedings began, and instructed the Presbytery to record the irregularity, acknowledge his new membership, and remove his name from the roll.

cases.title: Myers v. Illiana Presbytery
precedent_refs: Dallison v. North Florida Presbytery, M30GA (2002), page 156, 160-161
TE Rhett Dodson, et. al. v. Ohio Presbytery, M48GA 2021, 2019-01, Page 649, at 663
cases.synopsis: TE Aaron Myers appealed Illiana Presbytery's decision to elevate his censures from indefinite suspension from office and from the Sacraments to excommunication and deposition, without any additional judicial process. The SJC sustained the appeal 21-1, holding that due process principles require some form of process before censure may be elevated, though the majority held that full de novo judicial process was not required; the sole dissent (RE Eggert) argued that Traditional Process under the BCO Rules of Discipline was required, and a concurring opinion (Sartorius) also argued for full BCO process.
